---
title: "BCB744 Biostatistics"
author: "A. J. Smit"
date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY/MM/DD"
---

# Welcome

The [Introduction to R](../intro_r/index.qmd) section gave you the tools: R and RStudio, data import and manipulation, **ggplot2** graphics, and reproducible documents in Quarto. In this section, I put those tools to work to enable and support statistical reasoning.

I start from the foundations of data description and uncertainty, and then move through formal inference statistics and into regression and model-based analysis. So, the workflow traces along what a practicing biologist actually does, which is to go from  a question to a defensible conclusion.

I emphasise choosing appropriate methods, checking assumptions, interpreting model output, and communicating results to a scientific audience according to the norms of our discipline.

Work through the material along the sequence shown in the timetable (see [BCB744 Module Overview](../BCB744_index.qmd)), participate in [ongoing assessment](../tasks/BCB744_Biostats_Self-Assessment.qmd), and prepare yourself for the final theory and integrated assessments.

# Important Links

-   [Course Synthesis](../../docs/cheatsheet-course-synthesis.pdf)
-   [Statistics Methods Cheatsheet](../../docs/cheatsheet-inferential-stats.pdf)
-   [[**Self-Assessments**](../tasks/BCB744_Biostats_Self-Assessment.qmd)]{.my-highlight}
-   [[**BCB744 Data**](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1h77GFshqdzb80AkaNVlRR_NMg5Ar4dmQ?usp=sharing)]{.my-highlight}
